key_insights.insight: Humanizing interactions, by sharing something vulnerable or cracking a joke, sets the tone for deeper connection.
key_insights.quote: when you go first in humanizing the interaction, it is the essence of leadership.

key_insights.insight: Authenticity creates unshakable trust over time, even when behavior could be potentially damaging.
key_insights.quote: when you do authenticity over a period of time it creates just unshakable trust

key_insights.insight: Charisma can manifest in five primary ways: high conviction, authenticity, humor, empathy, and energy.
key_insights.quote: The five types that I think of are high conviction, authentic, funny, empathetic, and energetic.

key_insights.insight: Interpreting ambiguous communications charitably diffuses potentially negative situations and can make the other person look unreasonable.
key_insights.quote: You want to interpret ambiguous communications charitably.

key_insights.insight: Defensiveness over trivial matters communicates insecurity; comfort is shown by not defending or by adding to the joke.
key_insights.quote: You are sub-communicating that's not a big deal by not defending it.

key_insights.insight: Replacing filler words with silence creates a vacuum that pulls attention and makes your speech more impactful.
key_insights.quote: If you can replace any filler word, any crutch word that you have, with silence, silence is a vacuum, and the cool thing about vacuums is that they pull attention to you.

key_insights.insight: Expanding your physical presence through confident body language and gestures commands attention.
key_insights.quote: The space that you fill is one captivating...You need to fill your own completely.

key_insights.insight: Confidence stems from recognizing that everyone is fundamentally the same, regardless of status, and connecting with them on shared human experiences.
key_insights.quote: You're not dealing with roles...You are dealing with people...they connect with over the same things that you connect with your friends over.

actionable_advice.advice: Before an interview, talk to people (Uber driver, security guard, etc.) to warm up and be more dynamic.
actionable_advice.how_to: Engage in brief, positive conversations with people you encounter before the interview to get your vocal cords moving and reduce anxiety.

actionable_advice.advice: Prepare 3-5 core stories from your career to answer behavioral interview questions.
actionable_advice.how_to: Identify significant moments in your career, structure them as stories with a beginning, middle, and end (with an "up, down, up" curve), and practice telling them.

actionable_advice.advice: Ask interviewers what you would need to accomplish in the role in a year to be considered a successful hire.
actionable_advice.how_to: At the end of the interview, ask: 'If this interview goes well and I'm hired, what would I need to have accomplished in one year for you to feel like it was a good decision?'

actionable_advice.advice: To get a raise, ask your boss what you need to achieve in the next 6-8 months to make it a 'no-brainer' to give you one.
actionable_advice.how_to: Schedule a meeting and ask: 'In order for me to get this raise six to eight months from now, what would I have had to have done for it to be a no-brainer obvious to you that this was valuable?'

actionable_advice.advice: Record yourself telling stories to identify and eliminate filler words like 'um' and 'uh'.
actionable_advice.how_to: Record yourself speaking, then watch the video to identify and count instances of filler words. Practice telling the same stories again, consciously replacing those words with silence.

memorable_stories: Steve Jobs convincing Mike Scully to leave Pepsi to 'change the world'.
Brie Larson's awkward interviews during the Captain Marvel press tour.
Ted Lasso's positive responses to ambiguous communication.
contrarian_claims: Don't review technical knowledge right before an interview; it's either in your head or it's not.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Most Replayed Moment: How To Be Charismatic and Gain the Edge in Any Room - Charlie Houpert
